title: Hydronic panel for healting and or cooling
abstract: A hydronic panel and system for heating and/or cooling including a plurality of contiguous channels. A first chamber located at a first end of the panel and includes an inlet and communicates with a first subset of the channels. A second chamber located at an opposite end of the panel and communicates with the first subset and with a second subset of the channels. A third chamber is located at the fir end of the panel, the third chamber communicates with the second subset of the channels and includes an outlet. Wherein water flows from the inlet into the first chamber, through the first subset of the channels, to the second chamber, through the second subset of the channels, into the third chamber and out the outlet. The system includes a source of heated and/or cooled water under sufficient pressure and a controller to control one or both of the temperature and flow rate of the water through the panel.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A hydronic panel for heating or cooling a space comprising: a plurality of contiguous channels; a first chamber at a first end of the panel and comprising an inlet and communicating with a first subset of the channels; a second chamber at an opposite end of the panel and communicating with the first subset and a second subset of the channels; and a third chamber at the first end of the panel, the third chamber comprising an outlet and communicating with the second subset of the channels; wherein heated or cooled water flows from the inlet into the first chamber, through the first subset of the channels, to the second chamber, through the second subset of the channels, into the third chamber and out the outlet, whereby the space is heated or cooled, respectively. 2. The invention of claim 1 wherein the first end is an upper end of the panel and the second end is a lower end of the panel, such that the water flows generally downward through the first subset of the channels and generally upward through the second subset of the channels. 3. The invention of claim 1, wherein the hydronic panel forms at least part of a vertical wall of a room. 4. The invention of claim 3 further comprising a heat conducting cover on the side of the hydronic panel facing the room. 5. The invention of claim 4, wherein the heat conducting cover includes a decorative outer surface suitable to be the visible surface of the wall. 6. The invention of claim 3 further comprising a layer of heat insulating and/or heat reflecting material on the side of the hydronic panel facing away from the room. 7. The invention of claim 3 wherein the hydronic wall panel in combination with one or more other hydronic wall panels comprises more than 50 percent of the surface of the wall. 8. The invention of claim 1, wherein the hydronic panel forms at least part of a ceiling. 9. The invention of claim 1, wherein the hydronic panel forms at least part of a floor of a room. 10. The invention of claim 1 wherein the inlet is adapted to receive heated water from a solar thermal panel. 11. The invention of claim 1 wherein the inlet is adapted to receive heated and/or cooled water from a geothermal heat exchange. 12. The invention of claim 1 wherein the heating and/or cooling output of the hydronic panel are controlled by adjusting one or both of the temperature of the water and the flow rate through the hydronic panel. 13. The invention of claim 1, wherein the first chamber and the third chamber are separated by a plug. 14. The invention of claim 1, wherein the first subset of the channels comprises less than ten percent of the total number of the channels. 15. The invention of claim 1 wherein adjacent channels are separated by a common wall. 16. The invention of claim 1, wherein the plurality of contiguous channels are hollow rectangular prisms with two dimensions measuring less than one inch. 17. The invention of claim 1, wherein the plurality of contiguous channels is formed from twinwall plastic. 18. The invention of claim 1, wherein the first and third chamber are provided in a first block attached to one end of the contiguous channels and wherein the second chamber is provided in a second block attached to the opposite end of the contiguous channels. 19. A system for heating and/or heating a room comprising: a hydronic panel comprising a plurality of contiguous channels; a first chamber at one end of the panel, comprising an inlet and communicating with a first subset of the channels; a second chamber at an opposite end of the panel, communicating with the first subset of the channels and communicating with a second subset of the channels; a third chamber at the one end of the panel, comprising an outlet and communicating with the second subset of the channels; a source of heated and/or cooled water under sufficient pressure to cause the water to flow through the panel; and a controller to control one or both of the temperature of the water and the flow rate of the water through the panel. 20. The invention of claim 19 further comprising at least one additional hydronic panel of similar construction and wherein the outlet of the hydronic panel is in fluid communication with the inlet of the at least one additional hydronic panel. 21. The invention of claim 20 wherein the hydronic panels form part of a vertical wall of the room. 22. The invention of claim 21 wherein the hydronic panels form at least 75% of the vertical wall. 23. The invention of claim 19 comprising a solar thermal panel that provides the source of heated water and comprising a pump configured to pump water through the solar thermal panel. 24. The invention of claim 23 wherein the thermal panel is located on a roof. 25. The invention of claim 19 comprising a geothermal heat exchange to provide the source of heated and/or cooled water. 26. The invention of claim 25 further comprising a solar thermal panel, and wherein the system is configured to is pre-heat the water by the geothermal heat exchange and then heat the water further by the solar thermal panel. 27. The invention of claim 19 wherein adjacent channels are separated from each other by a common wall. 28. The invention of claim 19 wherein the plurality of contiguous channels are hollow rectangular prisms with two dimensions measuring less than one inch. 29. The invention of claim 19 wherein the plurality of contiguous channels is formed from twinwall plastic. 30. The invention of claim 29, wherein the first and third chamber are formed in one block attached to one otherwise open end of the twinwall plastic and wherein the second chamber is formed in another block attached to the other otherwise open end. 31. The invention of claim 29 wherein some of the channels are blocked by plugs at both ends to thereby direct the flow of water through the panel.
